title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer identifies that both the `db_sync` and `bootstrap` Jobs in the Keystone reconciler are missing `keystone.conf` ConfigMap volume mounts, meaning neither Job can read the database connection string and both will permanently fail with `RequiredOptError`. The fix is to add volume/volumeMount entries for the config ConfigMap to both Job specs (matching the pattern already used by the Deployment), and ensure `reconcileConfig` runs before `reconcileDatabase` so the ConfigMap exists when the db_sync Job launches.

code_comments.body:
**`db_sync` Job has no `keystone.conf` — will permanently fail**

`buildDBSyncJob` creates a Job with `keystone-manage db_sync` but no volume or environment variable provides a database connection. `keystone-manage db_sync` reads `[database] connection` from `keystone.conf` via oslo.config; without it, the command exits immediately with `oslo_config.cfg.RequiredOptError`.

This is compounded by the reconcile ordering: `reconcileDatabase` runs before `reconcileConfig`, so the config ConfigMap does not yet exist when db_sync first runs. Even on subsequent reconcile iterations the ConfigMap would exist, but the Job spec still doesn't mount it. After exhausting its 4 retries the Job enters the permanently-failed state, `job.RunJob` returns `ErrJobFailed`, `DatabaseReady` is set to `False` forever, and the entire reconcile chain is permanently blocked.

The db_sync Job spec needs a volume mounting the config ConfigMap and a matching `VolumeMount` on the container, similar to how the Deployment mounts the `config` volume. Alternatively, the DB connection URL can be injected as an env var (e.g. `OS_DATABASE_CONNECTION`). Either way, `reconcileConfig` must run before — or produce a minimal config for — `reconcileDatabase`, or `buildDBSyncJob` must construct the connection URL directly from the Keystone spec and inject it as an env var without relying on the ConfigMap.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go
Line: 163-184

Comment:
**`db_sync` Job has no `keystone.conf` — will permanently fail**

`buildDBSyncJob` creates a Job with `keystone-manage db_sync` but no volume or environment variable provides a database connection. `keystone-manage db_sync` reads `[database] connection` from `keystone.conf` via oslo.config; without it, the command exits immediately with `oslo_config.cfg.RequiredOptError`.

This is compounded by the reconcile ordering: `reconcileDatabase` runs before `reconcileConfig`, so the config ConfigMap does not yet exist when db_sync first runs. Even on subsequent reconcile iterations the ConfigMap would exist, but the Job spec still doesn't mount it. After exhausting its 4 retries the Job enters the permanently-failed state, `job.RunJob` returns `ErrJobFailed`, `DatabaseReady` is set to `False` forever, and the entire reconcile chain is permanently blocked.

The db_sync Job spec needs a volume mounting the config ConfigMap and a matching `VolumeMount` on the container, similar to how the Deployment mounts the `config` volume. Alternatively, the DB connection URL can be injected as an env var (e.g. `OS_DATABASE_CONNECTION`). Either way, `reconcileConfig` must run before — or produce a minimal config for — `reconcileDatabase`, or `buildDBSyncJob` must construct the connection URL directly from the Keystone spec and inject it as an env var without relying on the ConfigMap.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: buildDBSyncJob now accepts a configMapName parameter and includes a Volume referencing the config ConfigMap and a VolumeMount at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/ on the db-sync container. reconcileDatabase's signature was updated to accept configMapName. In keystone_controller.go, reconcileConfig was moved before reconcileDatabase (with an explanatory comment) so the ConfigMap exists when db_sync runs, and configMapName is passed through. All database tests were updated to pass configMapName and new assertions verify the volume mount and ConfigMap reference are present.

code_comments.body:
**Bootstrap Job missing `keystone.conf` mount — DB connection unavailable**

`buildBootstrapJob` constructs the `keystone-manage bootstrap` Job with the admin password injected via env var and all endpoint URLs passed as command-line args, but no volume mounts the `keystone.conf` ConfigMap. `keystone-manage bootstrap` writes the admin user, service, and endpoint records directly to the database; it requires a `[database] connection` in `keystone.conf` to establish that connection.

By the time `reconcileBootstrap` is called the ConfigMap produced by `reconcileConfig` exists, but without a `volumes` entry referencing it and a matching `volumeMounts` entry in the container, the file is not present inside the pod. The bootstrap Job will fail with the same oslo.config `RequiredOptError` as the db_sync Job.

The Job spec needs a `volumes` entry for the config ConfigMap (using the `configMapName` returned by `reconcileConfig`) and a corresponding `volumeMounts` entry mounting it at `/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/` — the same pattern used by the Deployment. To do this, `buildBootstrapJob` must accept `configMapName` as a parameter, and `reconcileBootstrap` must receive it (analogous to how `reconcileDeployment` already receives `configMapName`).

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go
Line: 63-104

Comment:
**Bootstrap Job missing `keystone.conf` mount — DB connection unavailable**

`buildBootstrapJob` constructs the `keystone-manage bootstrap` Job with the admin password injected via env var and all endpoint URLs passed as command-line args, but no volume mounts the `keystone.conf` ConfigMap. `keystone-manage bootstrap` writes the admin user, service, and endpoint records directly to the database; it requires a `[database] connection` in `keystone.conf` to establish that connection.

By the time `reconcileBootstrap` is called the ConfigMap produced by `reconcileConfig` exists, but without a `volumes` entry referencing it and a matching `volumeMounts` entry in the container, the file is not present inside the pod. The bootstrap Job will fail with the same oslo.config `RequiredOptError` as the db_sync Job.

The Job spec needs a `volumes` entry for the config ConfigMap (using the `configMapName` returned by `reconcileConfig`) and a corresponding `volumeMounts` entry mounting it at `/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/` — the same pattern used by the Deployment. To do this, `buildBootstrapJob` must accept `configMapName` as a parameter, and `reconcileBootstrap` must receive it (analogous to how `reconcileDeployment` already receives `configMapName`).

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: buildBootstrapJob now accepts a configMapName parameter and includes a Volume referencing the config ConfigMap and a VolumeMount at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/ on the bootstrap container. reconcileBootstrap's signature was updated to accept configMapName, and keystone_controller.go passes configMapName to reconcileBootstrap. All bootstrap tests were updated to pass configMapName and new assertions verify the volume mount and ConfigMap reference are present.

resolution_summary: Both review comments about missing keystone.conf ConfigMap mounts were fully addressed. The db_sync and bootstrap Job specs now include volume/volumeMount entries that mount the config ConfigMap at /etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/. The reconcile ordering in keystone_controller.go was restructured so that reconcileConfig runs before reconcileDatabase (fixing the ordering bug), and both reconcileDatabase and reconcileBootstrap now accept and propagate the configMapName parameter. Additionally, reconcileFernetKeys was moved before reconcileConfig in keystone_controller.go because reconcileConfig depends on fernet keys being present, making the reordering necessary to support the config-before-database change. All corresponding test files (reconcile_database_test.go, reconcile_bootstrap_test.go, keystone_controller_test.go) were updated to pass the configMapName parameter and verify the volume mounts.
